prs.title: fix: sync community meeting schedule with homepage (#2381)
prs.body:
**What kind of change does this PR introduce?**
Bug fix — corrected the Open Community Working Meetings schedule text on the community page to match the homepage and official Google Calendar.

**Issue Number:**
- Closes #2381


**Summary**
The community page (`/community`) displayed an incorrect Open Community Working Meetings schedule that conflicted with the homepage. Updated the text in `pages/community/index.page.tsx` to match the homepage and the official Google Calendar: "every third Monday of the month at 12:00 PT".

**Does this PR introduce a breaking change?**
No breaking changes. This is a text-only update.

# Checklist

Please ensure the following tasks are completed before submitting this pull request.

- [x] Read, understood, and followed the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/json-schema-org/website/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).

prs.body: Closes Issue #55 ## PR Title Fix bullet alignment in "How Analysis Works" section ## Description This PR resolves the misalignment issue of bullet points and text in the **"How Analysis Works"** section. ## Changes Made - Fixed vertical alignment of bullet points with corresponding text - Ensured consistent spacing between icons and text - Applied flex alignment improvements for better layout handling - Prevented bullet distortion when text wraps across multiple lines ## Before - Bullet points were slightly misaligned with text - Inconsistent spacing affected readability - Layout looked uneven, especially for multi-line text ## After - Bullet points are properly aligned with text baseline - Consistent spacing across all list items - Improved readability and cleaner UI appearance leaner UI appearance ## Why This Change? Proper alignment enhances visual consistency and improves the overall user experience. This fix ensures the section looks polished and consistent with the rest of the application.

prs.title: Move callback validation to __init__
prs.body:
## 📌 Summary

This PR fixes a bug where the `@validate_callback` decorator applied at
class definition time failed to validate overridden `on_loop_start` or
`on_loop_end` methods in subclasses of `CallBack`.

---

## 🐛 Problem

In the original code, `@validate_callback` was applied as a class-level
decorator:
```python
@validate_callback
class Deviance(CallBack):
    ...
```

This means validation ran **once at import time** on the parent class
methods only. If a user subclassed `Deviance` and overrode `on_loop_start`,
the new method was **never validated**:
```python
# This override was NEVER validated — silent bug!
class MyCallback(Deviance):
    def on_loop_start(self, gam, y, mu, some_invalid_arg):
        return 42
```

This could silently pass through and only crash at **runtime during model
fitting**, with a confusing error unrelated to the real cause.

---

## ✅ Fix

Moved validation into `CallBack.__init__` so it runs at **instance
creation time** on the actual instance — including any overridden methods:
```python
class CallBack(Core):
    def __init__(self, name=None):
        super(CallBack, self).__init__(name=name)
        validate_callback(self)  # ← runs on every instance, catches overrides
```

Also removed `@validate_callback` decorators from all subclass definitions
(`Deviance`, `Accuracy`, `Diffs`, `Coef`) since validation is now handled
automatically by the base class.

---

## 📂 Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `pygam/callbacks.py` | Added `validate_callback(self)` in `CallBack.__init__` |
| `pygam/callbacks.py` | Removed `@validate_callback` from `Deviance`, `Accuracy`, `Diffs`, `Coef` |

---

## 🔍 Before vs After

| | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| When validation runs | Once at import time | Every instance creation |
| Parent class methods validated | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |
| Subclass overrides validated | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| User-defined subclasses validated | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| Invalid signature caught early | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |

---

## 🧪 How to Test
```python
from pygam.callbacks import Deviance

# ✅ Valid subclass — should work fine
class GoodCallback(Deviance):
    def on_loop_start(self, gam, y, mu):
        return 99

cb = GoodCallback()  # No error


# ❌ Invalid subclass — should now raise AssertionError at instantiation
class BadCallback(Deviance):
    def on_loop_start(self, gam, y, mu, some_invalid_arg):
        return 42

cb = BadCallback()
# AssertionError: CallBack cannot reference: some_invalid_arg
```

---

## 🔗 Related Issue

Closes #[ISSUE_NUMBER]

---

## 📝 Notes

- The `validate_callback` function itself is unchanged — only **when**
  it is called has changed.
- The `_validated` flag inside `validate_callback` ensures there is
  **no double validation** if the function is somehow called twice on
  the same instance.
- This is a **non-breaking change** — all existing callbacks and
  correctly written subclasses will continue to work exactly as before.

prs.title: fix: open links in new tab
prs.body:
## Description

This PR fixes a broken link to the OpenJS Foundation Code of Conduct in `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` that was returning a 404 error.

## Changes

- Updated the OpenJS Foundation Code of Conduct URL in `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` with the correct working link

## Motivation

The existing link to the OpenJS Foundation Code of Conduct was returning a 404 (page not found) error, meaning users clicking on it would land on a broken page. This fix replaces it with the correct URL so users can access the referenced document.

## Related Issue

Fixes json-schema-org/website#<issue-number>

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation update

## Testing

- [x] Verified the updated link resolves correctly and does not return a 404

prs.title: fix: align icon and text in Join Discussions button
prs.body: ## 📋 Description Fixed the misalignment of the icon and text in the "Join Discussions" button on the Roadmap page. ## 🐛 Problem The social icon and the "Join Discussions" text were not aligned on the same line. This was caused by a redundant `` wrapper around `{children}` inside the `LoadingButton` component in `Spinner.tsx`. Since the `` lacked flex properties, the icon and text were treated as inline elements and aligned poorly. ## ✅ Fix Removed the redundant `` wrapper from around `{children}` in the `LoadingButton` component. The icon and text are now direct children of the flex container (`flex items-center justify-center space-x-2`), ensuring they stay perfectly centered on a single line. ## 📁 Files Changed - `src/components/Spinner.tsx` ## 📸 Screenshots - Before - After | Icon and text misaligned / stacking | Icon and text on same line ✅ | ## 🔗 Related Issue Closes #52

This ensures: - **Style Consistency** — Matches the `24x24` viewBox and stroke-weight of existing Lucide icons used across the project - **Zero Dependencies** — No additional packages required, keeping bundle size optimized ## Screenshots - Before : - After : ### ♿ Accessibility Improvement Updated the `title` attribute from `"Twitter"` to `"X"` for accurate screen reader announcements and improved semantic correctness. ### 🧹 Import Cleanup Removed the unused `Twitter` icon from the `lucide-react` import list, resolving linting warnings and keeping the codebase clean. ## Testing - [ ] Footer X icon renders correctly across screen sizes - [ ] Hover effects and transitions work as expected - [ ] No TypeScript or linting errors - [ ] Bundle size unaffected

prs.title: fix: patch landscape2 links to open in new tab
prs.body:
## Problem

`landscape2` builds the site from `guide.yml` and converts Markdown links to HTML `<a>` tags, but has these hard limitations:

- Strips `target="_blank"` even if written as raw HTML in `guide.yml`
- Strips `<script>` tags entirely
- External links get extra processing that was breaking rendering and showing raw HTML in the browser

There is no way to make `landscape2` output `target="_blank"` links — it's a built-in limitation of the tool.

## Solution

Since `guide.yml` itself can't fix this, we patch the **generated output** (`build/index.html`) after `landscape2` finishes building.

### What `build.sh` does

1. Runs `landscape2 build` — exactly as before
2. Uses Python to inject a small JS snippet into the bottom of `build/index.html`

The injected script uses a `MutationObserver` — a browser API that watches for DOM changes. Since the landscape2 site is a **SPA**, guide content is rendered dynamically after page load. The observer detects new content in the DOM and immediately sets `target="_blank"` on every link.

## Summary

| | |
|---|---|
| **Problem** | `landscape2` sanitizes and strips `target="_blank"` |
| **Fix** | Patch `build/index.html` after the build using Python |
| **Mechanism** | A `MutationObserver` in JS sets `target="_blank"` on all links dynamically |
| **When to run** | Use `

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8714d864-962b-4eba-8b4d-f21d8a0d89e7

bash build.sh` instead of `landscape2 build ...` every time you rebuild |

prs.title: fix: open card link in new tab using target _blank
prs.body:
Fixes #2290 

**What kind of change does this PR introduce?**
Bug fix — external links were opening in the same tab instead of a new tab.

**What does this PR do?**
Added `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"` to all external anchor
tags so they open in a new browser tab without navigating the user away from
the current page.

# Checklist
- [x] I have tested the changes locally
- [x] External links now open in a new tab
- [x] No existing functionality is broken
- [x] `rel="noopener noreferrer"` added for security

prs.title: fix: accordion expand icon now changes to × on toggle
prs.body:
**What kind of change does this PR introduce?**
Bug fix

**Description**
Fixes #2278

When clicking the `+` icon to expand an accordion card, the icon was not
updating to reflect the expanded state. This was caused by `rotate-45`
being applied on the parent div instead of the icon span.

**Changes Made**
- Removed `rotate-45` from the parent icon container div
- Added `inline-block` and `transition-transform duration-200` to the span
- Added `select-none` to prevent text selection on click

# Checklist
- [x] My code follows the style guidelines of this project
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [x] My changes don't require a change to the documentation

labels.name: 🐛 Bug

issues.body: ### Describe the bug The meeting schedule for Open Community Working Meetings is described differently on two pages of the website, creating confusion for community members trying to find the correct time. ## Pages affected - **Homepage** (`/`): _"Open Community Working Meetings are every third Monday of the month at 12:00 PT"_ - **Community page** (`/community`): _"Open Community Working Meetings are every Monday at 14:00 PT"_ These descriptions conflict on two points: 1. **Frequency** — "every third Monday" vs "every Monday" 2. **Time** — 12:00 PT vs 14:00 PT ### Steps To Reproduce 1. Visit https://json-schema.org/ and scroll to the "Community Meetings & Events" section 2. Visit https://json-schema.org/community and read the meeting schedule description 3. Compare the two — they do not match ### Expected Behavior Both pages should display the same, accurate schedule that matches the [official Google Calendar](https://calendar.google.com/calendar/embed?src=info%40json-schema.org). ### Screenshots ### Device Information [optional] ```markdown - OS:MacOS - Browser:Chrome - version: ``` ### Are you working on this issue? Yes ### Do you think this work might require an [Architectural Decision Record (ADR)]? (significant or noteworthy) Yes

issues.title: [BUG] @validate_callback decorator applied at class level doesn't validate overridden methods in subclasses
issues.body:
---

## Describe the Bug

In `pygam/callbacks.py`, the `@validate_callback` decorator is applied directly
on the class definitions at module load time:
```python
@validate_callback
class Deviance(CallBack):
    ...
```

Inside `validate_callback`, the wrapping is done using `setattr` on 
`on_loop_start` / `on_loop_end` methods **once**, when the file is first 
imported. This means if a user subclasses `Deviance` (or any other callback) 
and overrides `on_loop_start` or `on_loop_end`, the new method will **not** 
be wrapped by `validate_callback_data`.

---

## Steps to Reproduce
```python
from pygam.callbacks import Deviance, validate_callback_data

class MyCallback(Deviance):
    def on_loop_start(self, gam, y, mu, some_invalid_arg):
        return 42  # This override is never validated

cb = MyCallback()
# No error is raised, even though the method signature is wrong
# The validation was only applied to Deviance, not MyCallback
```

---

## Expected Behavior

Subclasses that override `on_loop_start` or `on_loop_end` should either:
- Be automatically re-validated when instantiated, **or**
- Raise a clear error if their method signature references arguments
  not available in the callback data vars dict

---

## Actual Behavior

No validation occurs on overridden methods in subclasses. The bug can 
silently pass through and only fail at **runtime during model fitting**, 
with a confusing error message unrelated to the real cause.

---

## Root Cause

`validate_callback` uses `setattr` to wrap methods on the **instance or 
class passed to it**. Since `@validate_callback` is applied at class 
definition time, it only wraps the methods of the decorated class — 
not any future subclasses.
```python
# This only wraps Deviance.on_loop_start once at import time
@validate_callback
class Deviance(CallBack):
    ...
```

---

## Suggested Fix

Move validation to `__init_subclass__` or to the `CallBack` base class 
`__init__` method, so that every new subclass or instance is validated 
at creation time:
```python
class CallBack(Core):
    def __init__(self, name=None):
        super().__init__(name=name)
        validate_callback(self)  # validate at instance creation
```

This ensures all subclasses — including user-defined ones — are validated 
regardless of when or how they are created.

---

## Environment

| Detail | Info |
|--------|------|
| File | `pygam/callbacks.py` |
| Affected Classes | `Deviance`, `Accuracy`, `Diffs`, `Coef` and any user-defined subclasses |
| Decorator | `@validate_callback` |
| Impact | All versions using the current decorator pattern |

---

## Labels

`bug` `callbacks` `subclassing` `validation`

issues.title: 🐛 Bug Report: Footer "Getting Started" Link Returns 404
issues.body: ## 📋 Description The "Getting Started" link in the footer's Documentation section returns a 404 error when clicked, indicating a broken or misconfigured route. ## 🖥️ Location Footer → Documentation Section → "Getting Started" link ## 🔄 Current Behavior Clicking "Getting Started" in the footer navigates to a page that returns a 404 Not Found error. ## ✅ Expected Behavior Clicking "Getting Started" should navigate the user to the correct Getting Started documentation page without any errors. ## 🛠️ Possible Fix - Verify the href path of the "Getting Started" anchor tag in the footer component - Ensure the corresponding route/page exists in the codebase - Check for any typos in the route path ## 📸 Screenshots ## 🏷️ Labels - `bug` - `documentation` - `good first issue`

issues.title: 🐛 Bug Report: "Back to Blog" Button is Sticky/Fixed to the Bottom of the Page
issues.body: ## 📋 Description The "← Back to Blog" button at the end of the blog post page appears to be fixed/sticky to the bottom of the viewport instead of being statically positioned within the page content flow. ## 🖥️ Location Blog Post Page → Bottom of article → "← Back to Blog" button ## 🔄 Current Behavior The "← Back to Blog" button is fixed to the bottom of the screen, overlapping the page content and not scrolling naturally with the rest of the page. ## ✅ Expected Behavior The "← Back to Blog" button should be statically positioned within the normal document flow — appearing naturally after the article content, above the footer, without being sticky or fixed to the viewport. ## 📸 Screenshots ## 🏷️ Labels - `bug` - `UI/UX` - `good first issue`

issues.title: 🐛 Bug Report: "View on GitHub" Button Opens in Same Tab Instead of New Tab
issues.body: ## 📋 Description Clicking the "View on GitHub" button on the homepage navigates the user away from the Newgate JS website by opening the GitHub repository in the same browser tab. It should open in a new tab to preserve the user's current session on the site. ## 🖥️ Location Home Page → Hero Section → "View on GitHub" button ## 🔄 Current Behavior Clicking "View on GitHub" opens the GitHub repository (https://github.com/XplnHUB/newgate) in the same tab, navigating the user away from the website. ## ✅ Expected Behavior Clicking "View on GitHub" should open the GitHub repository in a new browser tab, keeping the Newgate JS website open in the background. ## 🛠️ Possible Fix Add `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"` to the anchor tag of the button: View on GitHub ## 📸 Screenshots https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/10d4bbd8-6fc2-48c2-94c4-cd4b9814a543 ## 🏷️ Labels - `bug` - `UI/UX` - `good first issue`

issues.title: 🐛 Bug Report: List Bullet Points Misaligned with Text in "How Analysis Works" Section
issues.body: ## 🐛 Bug Report: List Bullet Points Misaligned with Text in "How Analysis Works" Section ## 📋 Description In the "How Analysis Works" section, the bullet points (dots) are not properly aligned with their corresponding text. The dots appear to be vertically misaligned, making the list look inconsistent and visually unpolished. ## 🖥️ Location Home Page / Features Page → "How Analysis Works" section ## 🔄 Current Behavior The bullet point dots are misaligned with the text — they appear to sit at a different vertical position than the text baseline, causing an off-center look. ## ✅ Expected Behavior The bullet point dots should be perfectly vertically centered with their corresponding text on the same line. ## 📸 Screenshots ## 🏷️ Labels - `bug` - `UI/UX` - `good first issue`

issues.title: 🚀 Feature Request: Add a Button for "View Full Roadmap"
issues.body: ## 📋 Description The "View Full Roadmap" link on the Home page is currently a plain text link with a small arrow icon. It would be better to replace it with a proper styled button, consistent with the design language of the rest of the site (similar to the "View Full Implementation Guide" button above it). ## 🖼️ Current Behavior The "View Full Roadmap" is displayed as a plain text link (›), which is easy to miss and doesn't stand out visually. ## ✅ Expected Behavior A styled button like: [ View Full Roadmap › ] Similar to the "View Full Implementation Guide" button — with a visible border, hover effect, and consistent typography. ## 💡 Motivation - Improves visual consistency across the page - Makes the CTA more prominent and accessible - Better UX for users to navigate to the Roadmap ## 📸 Screenshots ## 🖥️ Page Home Page → "What's Coming Next" section

Navigate to the Roadmap page 2. Observe the "Join Discussions" button under "Help Shape Our Roadmap" 3. Notice the icon and text are not on the same line ### Suggested Fix Add proper flex alignment to the button: ```css display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; ``` ### Screenshots [Attach screenshot here] ### Environment - Browser: Chrome / Safari - Screen size: Desktop

issues.title: 🐦 Update Twitter Icon to New X Logo
issues.body: **Describe the Issue** The social media icon in the **Connect** section of the footer currently displays the old Twitter bird logo. Twitter has officially rebranded to **X**, and the icon should reflect this change. **Location** - Page: Footer → `CONNECT` section - Component: Social media icon links **Current Behavior** The footer displays the legacy Twitter bird icon. **Expected Behavior** The icon should be replaced with the official **X (formerly Twitter)** logo to stay consistent with the current branding. **Suggested Fix** Replace the existing Twitter icon with the X logo from an icon library that supports it, such as: - `fa-x-twitter` from **Font Awesome 6.4+** - `RiTwitterXFill` from **react-icons** - Or use the official SVG from X's brand kit **Additional Context** This is a minor UI update but important for keeping the site's branding accurate and up to date. Happy to submit a PR for this if it gets approved! 🙌

issues.title: Bug : Discussion link returns 404
issues.body: ### 📌 Description Clicking on the **"Discussions"** link in the Resources section redirects to a **404 NOT_FOUND** page instead of the intended destination. ### 🔍 Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to the website 2. Scroll to the **Resources** section in the footer 3. Click on **"Discussions"** 4. Observe the error page ### ❗ Expected Behavior The "Discussions" link should: * Redirect to the project's **GitHub Discussions page** **OR** * Open a valid internal `/discussions` route ### 🚫 Actual Behavior * A **404 NOT_FOUND** error is displayed * Indicates that the route does not exist or is incorrectly configured ### 💡 Possible Cause * Missing route (`/discussions`) in the project * Incorrect link path used in the footer * GitHub Discussions URL not properly configured ### 🌍 Environment * Deployment: Vercel * Framework: Next.js * Browser: (e.g., Chrome) --- ### 🙌 Additional Notes This affects navigation usability and may confuse users trying to access community discussions.

issues.title: **Bug Report: [source] links open in the same tab instead of a new tab**
issues.body:
## Description

In the API Reference documentation, clicking the `[source]` links (which point to external GitHub URLs like `https://github.com/dswah/pyGAM/blob/...`) opens in the **same browser tab**, causing users to navigate away from the documentation page.

## Expected Behavior

External links such as `[source]` should open in a **new tab** (`target="_blank"`) so users don't lose their place in the documentation.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to the API Reference page (e.g., `ExpectileGAM` section)
2. Click any `[source]` link on the right side of a method/function
3. Observe that the GitHub source file opens in the **same tab**

## Environment

- Documentation site: https://pygam.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/_autosummary/pygam.pygam.ExpectileGAM.html(or equivalent)
- Browser: [your browser here]
- OS: [your OS here]

## Suggested Fix

Ensure that external `[source]` links include `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"` attributes. This may need to be configured in the Sphinx theme or documentation build settings.

## Additional Context

Screenshot attached showing the `[source]` link on the `generate_X_grid` method pointing to `https://github.com/dswah/pyGAM/blob/b6a48ab/pygam.py#L1437`.

issues.title: Code blocks in documentation lack visible boundaries on mobile devices
issues.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Code/command blocks in the Bootstrap documentation are not visually distinct on mobile devices. The boundaries (borders/background contrast) are not clearly visible, making the blocks appear like regular text instead of formatted code sections.

### Reduced test cases

### Steps to reproduce
1. Open Bootstrap documentation website
2. Navigate to the "Install via package manager" section
3. Observe the command blocks (e.g., npm install, gem install)

### Expected behavior
Code blocks should have clear visual separation (e.g., visible borders, background contrast, or padding) so they are easily distinguishable from normal text.

### Actual behavior
The code blocks blend into the background, and their boundaries are difficult to notice, especially on smaller screens.

### Impact
This reduces readability and may confuse beginners who cannot easily identify commands.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cd3dc999-f508-482d-b8db-b2c988f96b79)
### Suggested fix
Improve styling of code blocks by:
- Adding stronger border contrast OR
- Enhancing background color difference OR
- Increasing padding/box shadow for better visibility

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.1.0

issues.title: fix: back-to-top button jumps instantly — add smooth scroll transition

labels.name: 🐛 Bug
labels.description: Something isn't working

issues.body:
### Describe the bug

The back-to-top (↑) button on the landscape guide page teleports 
the user instantly to the top of the page with zero animation. 
This feels abrupt and disorienting, especially on longer sections 
where the user has scrolled far down.

A smooth scroll transition is a standard UX expectation on modern 
web pages — most users expect a gentle glide back to the top rather 
than a sudden jump. This is a small change with a noticeable 
improvement to the reading experience.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Open the landscape guide page in any browser
2. Click on any category/section from the left sidebar
   (e.g. one with long content)
3. Scroll down manually until the ↑ back-to-top button
   appears in the bottom-right corner
4. Click the ↑ button
5. Page instantly jumps to the top with no animation

### Expected Behavior

Page should smoothly scroll back to the top with a transition 
(~300–500ms ease), giving the user visual continuity.

## Actual Behavior

Page teleports directly to the top — no scroll animation, 
no easing, abrupt UX.

## Suggested Fix
Since `landscape2` strips `<style>` tags from `guide.yml`, 
this should be applied the same way as the `target="_blank"` 
patch in #130 — by injecting it into `build/index.html` 
via `build.sh` after the build.

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/174d34c0-aabc-4e78-ae7d-865d9e8e4ba6

### Device Information [optional]

```markdown
- OS:MacOS
- Browser:Chrome
- version:
```

### Are you working on this issue?

Yes

### Do you think this work might require an [Architectural Decision Record (ADR)]? (significant or noteworthy)

Yes

issues.title: Bug Report: External Links Open in Same Tab
issues.body:
### Describe the bug

External links on the Install page (and possibly other pages) open in the same browser tab instead of opening in a new tab. This disrupts the user's navigation flow, as clicking an external link navigates them away from the documentation entirely.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce


1. Go to the Install page
2. Click on any external link (e.g., "virtual environment (venv)" or "PyPI")
3. Observe that the link opens in the same tab

### Expected Behavior

External links (links pointing outside the docs site) should open in a new tab (`target="_blank"`) so users don't lose their place in the documentation.

### Actual Behavior

Clicking external links navigates away from the current page in the same tab.

**Suggested Fix:**
Add `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"` to all external anchor tags. If using Sphinx, this can be configured via a JavaScript snippet or an extension like `sphinx-new-tab-link`.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f9136cb1-2ee8-49d5-bf7d-b495461b2b46

### Environment

```shell
Not applicable — this is a documentation website bug, not a library/code issue.

Browser: Chrome
OS: MacOS
```

issues.title: 🐛 Bug: Bronze Sponsor Link Does Not Scroll to Sponsors Section on Homepage

labels.name: 🐛 Bug
labels.description: Indicates that the issue is a bug or defect.

labels.name: Status: Triage
labels.description: This is the initial status for an issue that requires triage.

issues.body:
### Describe the bug

> When clicking the link to the "sponsors" section on the homepage (https://json-schema.org) from the Bronze Sponsors documentation page, the page navigates to the homepage but does not scroll down to the "sponsors" section. Instead, it lands at the top of the page.

### Steps To Reproduce

> - Go to the Sponsors documentation page: https://json-schema.org/docs/introduction/sponsors
> - Under "Benefits of being a bronze sponsor", click the link: https://json-schema.org
> - Observe the behavior — the browser navigates to the homepage but does not anchor/scroll to the "sponsors" section.

### Expected Behavior

> Clicking the link should navigate to the homepage AND automatically scroll/jump to the "sponsors" section (e.g., via an anchor like `https://json-schema.org/#sponsors`).

**Actual Behavior**
> The user is taken to the top of the homepage with no scrolling to the sponsors section.


**Possible Fix**
> The link likely needs an anchor fragment appended, for example:
`https://json-schema.org/#sponsors`
(The exact anchor ID should be verified against the homepage HTML.)

### Screenshots

issues.title: 🐛 Bug: Clicking "Name" sort in one section sorts all sections globally

labels.name: 🐛 Bug
labels.description: Indicates that the issue is a bug or defect.

labels.name: Status: Triage
labels.description: This is the initial status for an issue that requires triage.

issues.body:
### Describe the bug

On the Implementations page, there are multiple tool category sections (e.g., Validator, Annotations, Code Generation, etc.), each with their own table and a sortable Name column header.
When the Name sort is clicked in any one section, the sort is incorrectly applied to every single section on the page simultaneously — instead of being scoped only to the section where the sort was triggered.

### Steps To Reproduce

- Go to the Tools page (which contains many category sections, each with a sortable table).
- Click the Name ↑ sort button in the header of any section (e.g., Validator).
- Observe all other sections on the page.

### Expected Behavior

- Clicking Name sort in the Validator section should reorder only the rows within the Validator table.
- All other sections on the page should remain completely unaffected and retain their own independent sort order.

Actual Behavior
- Clicking Name sort in any one section causes every section on the entire page to sort simultaneously.
- No section maintains its own independent sort state — they all share a single global sort state

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c1616bea-1943-41a6-97a4-a86b4414f6e6

### Device Information [optional]

```markdown
- OS:MacOS
- Browser:Chrome
- version:
```

issues.title: 🐛 Bug: Bug: Sorting by Name collapses the list loaded via "Load More"

labels.name: 🐛 Bug
labels.description: Indicates that the issue is a bug or defect.

labels.name: Status: Triage
labels.description: This is the initial status for an issue that requires triage.

issues.body:
### Describe the bug

After using **Load More** to expand the list, clicking the **Name** sort column causes the list to shorten back to its original size. Every time sorting is applied, the extra loaded items disappear.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Open the list view
2. Click **Load More** — additional items load successfully ✅
3. Click the **Name** column to sort
4. The list shortens back — extra items are gone ❌
5. Clicking **Name** sort again continues to keep the list short

### Expected Behavior

Clicking the **Name** sort should sort all currently visible items (including those loaded via Load More) **without reducing the list size**.

### Actual Behavior
Every time the **Name** sort is clicked, the list resets to its original shorter length, discarding all items brought in by Load More.

### Root Cause (suspected)
The sort action is likely triggering a fresh API/data fetch with a default page size (e.g., limit=10), overwriting the expanded list instead of sorting it in place.

### Possible Fix
- **Client-side sort:** Sort the existing in-memory array without re-fetching data
- **Server-side sort:** Include the total number of currently loaded items in the sort request (e.g., `limit=30` if 30 items are loaded) so the full set is returned sorted
- Avoid resetting pagination state when a sort is applied

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2bdebbef-d01a-4d65-a907-eee277f38193

### Device Information [optional]

```markdown
- OS:MacOS
- Browser:Chrome
- version:
```

### Are you working on this issue?

Yes

### Do you think this work might require an [Architectural Decision Record (ADR)]? (significant or noteworthy)

Yes

issues.title: 🐛 Bug: External links open in the same tab instead of a new tab

labels.name: 🐛 Bug
labels.description: Indicates that the issue is a bug or defect.

labels.name: Status: Accepted
labels.description: It's clear what the subject of the issue is about, and what the resolution should be.

issues.body:
### Describe the bug

When clicking on external links throughout the application, the destination page loads in the same browser tab, replacing the current page. Expected behavior is that external links should open in a new tab so users don't lose their current context/position.

### Steps To Reproduce

- Navigate to Home Page
- Click on any external link
- Observe that the page opens in the same tab

### Expected Behavior

External links should open in a new browser tab (i.e., using **target="_blank"** ).

### **Actual Behaviour**
Clicking an external link navigates away from the current page within the same tab.

### **Suggested Fix**
Add **target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"** to all external anchor tags. If links are rendered dynamically (e.g., from Markdown or a rich-text renderer), ensure the renderer is configured to apply these attributes to external URLs.

### Screenshots



https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8da0b665-8b43-4cee-927a-aa1ffbe3a1c3

### Device Information [optional]

```markdown
- OS:MacOS
- Browser:Chrome
- version:
```

Yes

Yes

issues.title: 🐛 Bug: Copy Button & Schema Label Missing Top/Right Margin in Code Blocks

labels.name: 🐛 Bug
labels.description: Indicates that the issue is a bug or defect.

labels.name: Status: Triage
labels.description: This is the initial status for an issue that requires triage.

issues.body: ### Describe the bug The copy icon (📋) and the "schema" / "data" label that appear in code blocks are positioned fixed/sticky to the top-right corner without any margin from the top and right edges of the code block container. This causes them to appear flush against the border, which looks visually unpolished and can also partially overlap the code content. ### Steps To Reproduce 1. Navigate to the "The basics" page in the Docs section. 2. Observe any code block (e.g., the empty schema `{}` block or the data examples below it). 3. Notice the copy icon and "schema"/"data" label are pinned with no spacing from the top-right corner of the code block. ### Expected Behavior The copy button and label should have consistent margin/padding from the top and right edges of the code block — e.g., `margin-top: 8px` and `margin-right: 8px` (or equivalent) — so they appear visually balanced and do not touch the border. ### **Actual Behaviour** The copy button and label are flush to the top-right with no visible margin, making the UI feel cramped. ### **Suggested Fix** Add top and right margin/padding to the sticky copy button and badge container inside code blocks: ```css .code-block-actions { margin-top: 8px; margin-right: 8px; } ``` ### Device Information [optional] - OS:MacOS - Browser:Chrome - version: ### Screenshots ### Are you working on this issue? Yes ### Do you think this work might require an [Architectural Decision Record (ADR)]? (significant or noteworthy) Yes

issues.title: Arrow icons misaligned and distracting hover animation on Developer Resources cards

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Something isn't working

issues.body:
## Description
On the "Important Links for Developers" page, the arrow icons on each resource card have two UX issues:

1. **Vertical alignment:** The arrows are not properly centered vertically within their cards, creating visual inconsistency
2. **Hover animation:** The forward movement of arrows on hover feels jarring and doesn't contribute to a smooth user experience


<!-- Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to '...'
2. Click on '....'
3. Scroll down to '....'
4. See error -->

## Expected behavior

- Arrows should be vertically centered within each card
- Hover effects should be more subtle 

  ## Current behavior

- Arrows appear slightly off-center
- On hover, arrows translate forward creating a distracting motion effect

## Suggested fix
Adjust CSS to properly center arrows using flexbox/grid alignment

- Consider replacing the translate animation with a more subtle hover state (opacity change, color shift, or remove animation entirely)

- **Operating System:** macOS
- **Browser:** Chrome

issues.title: 🐛 Bug: Footer collision and sidebar content overflow at 1032px width

labels.name: 🐛 Bug
labels.description: Indicates that the issue is a bug or defect.

labels.name: Status: Triage
labels.description: This is the initial status for an issue that requires triage.

issues.body: ### Describe the bug I've noticed two layout issues on the documentation site at a screen width of 1032px: **1. Footer Collision** The gradient footer element is colliding with the main content area. The footer appears to be positioned too high, causing it to overlap the "Need Help?" section and the community links. **2. Sidebar Content Overflow** The left sidebar contains an icon grid box (showing design/development icons) with the text "Design and Development tips in your inbox. Every weekday. ADS VIA CARBON". This content is overflowing its container boundaries. ### Steps To Reproduce 1. Visit the JSON Schema documentation site (https://json-schema.org/) 2. Open browser DevTools 3. Set viewport width to 1032px 4. Observe the footer and left sidebar ### Expected Behavior - The footer should be positioned below all content without overlapping - The sidebar content should be contained within its boundaries or handle overflow gracefully ### **Suggested Fix:** The footer's positioning or z-index might need adjustment, and the sidebar may need either: - A max-width constraint with overflow: hidden - Responsive font sizing - Better padding/margin management at this breakpoint ### Screenshots ### Device Information [optional] ```markdown - OS: MacOS - Browser: Google Chrome - version: - Screen width: 1032px ``` ### Are you working on this issue? Yes ### Do you think this work might require an [Architectural Decision Record (ADR)]? (significant or noteworthy) Yes

issues.title: 🐛 Bug: Clear Filters button missing hover effect in dark mode

labels.name: 🐛 Bug
labels.description: Indicates that the issue is a bug or defect.

labels.name: Status: Triage
labels.description: This is the initial status for an issue that requires triage.

issues.body:
### Describe the bug

The "Clear Filters" button lacks hover effects in dark mode, while it works properly in light mode. This creates an inconsistent user experience across themes.
Current Behavior

- **Light Mode**: Clear Filters button has a red/pink border and visible hover effects ✅
- **Dark Mode**: Clear Filters button appears as plain text with no hover effect or border styling ❌

### Steps To Reproduce

- Go to the JSON Schema Tools page
- Switch to dark mode
- Hover over the "Clear Filters" button
- Notice the lack of hover effect compared to light mode

### Expected Behavior

The Clear Filters button should have consistent hover effects in both light and dark modes, similar to how the Apply Filters button behaves.

### **Suggested Fix**
Add proper hover states for the Clear Filters button in dark mode CSS, including:

- Border styling (similar to light mode)
- Hover background color change
- Transition effects

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/983358a8-5cc9-4ae5-ae1d-13e268da46c2
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3337ff2d-94e3-47d1-b7a0-edd321f3eaa2

### Device Information [optional]

```markdown
- OS:MacOS
- Browser:Chrome
- version:
```

### Are you working on this issue?

Yes

### Do you think this work might require an [Architectural Decision Record (ADR)]? (significant or noteworthy)

Yes

issues.title: ✨ Enhancement: Add hover effect with background color change to "GROUP BY" filter buttons

labels.name: ✨ Enhancement
labels.description: Indicates that the issue suggests an improvement or new feature.

labels.name: Status: Triage
labels.description: This is the initial status for an issue that requires triage.

issues.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

Currently, the "GROUP BY" filter buttons (None, Tooling Type, Language) on the Tools page lack visual feedback when users hover over them. This makes it unclear which button the user is about to interact with, reducing the overall user experience.
### **Current Behavior:**
The three filter buttons ("None", "Tooling Type", "Language") displayed below the "Adding your tool" and "Try Bowtie" banners do not have any hover state styling. Users cannot visually distinguish which button they are about to click.
### **Proposed Enhancement:**
Add hover effects with distinct background color changes to the "GROUP BY" filter buttons to provide clear visual feedback and improve interactivity.
### **Expected Behavior:**

- When a user hovers their cursor over any of the three filter buttons, the background color should change to indicate interactivity
- The transition should be smooth and consistent with the existing design system
- The hover effect should be distinct from the active/selected state (currently "Tooling Type" appears to be selected with a blue background)

### **Benefits:**

- Improves user experience with immediate visual feedback
- Makes the interface more intuitive and interactive
- Maintains consistency with modern web design patterns
- Enhances overall polish of the tooling page

### Describe the solution you'd like

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a4e0943e-d7c9-4268-8364-bfab6b9bb19f

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/40fc02d3-dc4f-4fa5-bec3-552abf4147db

### Describe alternatives you've considered

- Add CSS hover states with appropriate background color transitions
- Ensure sufficient color contrast for accessibility 
- Maintain consistency across similar elements throughout the application
- Consider using smooth transitions for a polished effect (e.g., transition: background-color 0.3s ease)

### Additional context

_No response_

### Are you working on this?

Yes

issues.title: 🐛 Bug: Join Slack button on Community page returns 404 error

labels.name: 🐛 Bug
labels.description: Indicates that the issue is a bug or defect.

labels.name: Status: Triage
labels.description: This is the initial status for an issue that requires triage.

issues.body: ### Describe the bug The "Join Slack" button is currently non-functional and returns a 404 error when clicked. ### Actual Behavior Clicking the button leads to a 404 "Page Not Found" error. ### Possible Cause The Slack invite link may be expired, outdated, or incorrectly configured. ### Suggested Fix Update the button with a current/valid Slack invite link Consider using a non-expiring Slack invite link Verify the link is correctly configured in the codebase ### Additional Context This issue prevents new contributors from joining the community Slack workspace, which may reduce community engagement. The button is prominently displayed on the Community page with the text "Join our Slack to ask questions, get feedback on your projects, and connect with +5000 practitioners and experts." Screenshots: ### Steps To Reproduce 1. Navigate to the Community page (json-schema.org/community or the Community section 2. Click on the blue "Join Slack" button in the "Join the JSON Schema Slack workspace!" section 3. Observe the 404 "Page Not Found!" error with message "The page you were looking for doesn't exist." ### Expected Behavior The button should redirect users to the project's Slack workspace invitation page or a valid Slack invite link. ### Screenshots https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/725edd90-dc28-45cc-a7eb-ef81cd11db1f ### Device Information [optional] ```markdown - OS:Chrome 120 - Browser:macOS 14 - version: ``` ### Are you working on this issue? Yes ### Do you think this work might require an [Architectural Decision Record (ADR)]? (significant or noteworthy) Yes
